title: Day One
narrative: We launched with 7 classic games — bare bones, functional, ugly. The kind of thing you ship at 2am and immediately regret showing anyone. But it worked. Snake moved, Pong bounced, Tetris stacked. That was enough.

title: The Pivot
narrative: We looked at what we'd built and decided it wasn't enough. Games worked but they didn't feel like anything. So we wrote a pivot proposal — Arcade Evolution. The idea: take every game and give it a soul. Neon visuals, sound effects, particle systems, real game feel. We picked LittleJS as the engine, wrote creative direction specs for all 7 games, and started rebuilding from scratch.

title: Going Static
narrative: Ripped out Docker, the backend, nginx — all of it. The platform is just static files on GitHub Pages now. Simpler. Faster. No more 'backend offline' messages because there is no backend. Sometimes the best architecture decision is removing architecture.

title: Phase 3 — The Grind
narrative: Seven games. Each one rebuilt from the ground up with its own identity. Snake became Neon Serpent — glowing trails, combo system, 10 difficulty tiers. Pong became Volt Rally with electric effects. Breakout got prism shatter particles. Every game got zzfx sound effects, high score persistence, and a personality. The hardest was Pac-Man — ghost AI with four distinct personalities, fog of war, a real maze. But we shipped them all.

title: Phase 4 Begins
narrative: All 7 games rebuilt. Now it's about the platform itself — game selection redesign, settings UI, achievement system, security hardening. The games are fun. Time to make the wrapper worthy of them.
